directive.title: RFQ status transition API with audit log integration

auditor.summary: The implementation correctly implements the RFQ status transition state machine with atomic Drizzle transactions and immutable audit logging. All acceptance criteria are satisfied or partially satisfied. The code is well-structured with proper error handling, typed interfaces, and follows the spec closely. The main concern is the unresolved import path for the database client which could cause build failures. Minor type safety improvements could be made but don't block the build. The implementation meets EQS requirements for data lineage, financial integrity gating, and reliability.

top_issues.description: Import '@/lib/db' may not resolve if the database client export path differs from the expected location. The spec references 'lib/db/schema.ts' but the route imports from '@/lib/db' which could be a barrel export or a separate file. This could cause a build failure if the path doesn't exist.
The rfqId validation checks for falsy values but params.id from Next.js dynamic routes will always be a string (possibly empty). The check is redundant but harmless.
The error handling uses type assertions (error as IllegalTransitionError) which could mask runtime type mismatches. While not technically 'any', it's a minor type safety concern.
